parameters.content:
## Customer Support Call to Zendesk Ticket + AI Summary + Google Sheets Log — WayinVideo Transcription + GPT-4o-mini + Zendesk + Sheets

For customer support teams, customer success managers, and QA teams who record support calls and want to automatically log structured tickets in Zendesk — without manually listening to recordings or writing ticket notes after every call. Submit any support call recording URL via the form. WayinVideo Transcription API transcribes the full call with speaker labels and timestamps. GPT-4o-mini reads the transcript and extracts 10 structured fields: ticket subject, issue summary, root cause, resolution given, follow-up actions, key customer quotes, sentiment, priority, CSAT prediction (1–5), and tags. A Code node builds the formatted Zendesk ticket body with section headers, maps the priority to Zendesk values, and assembles all fields. An HTTP Request creates the Zendesk ticket via the REST API with the customer as requester. Google Sheets logs all 19 fields including the Zendesk Ticket ID and constructed Ticket URL.

## How it works
- **1. Form — Support Call + Details** collects the recording URL, agent name, customer name, customer email, issue category, call date, and product name
- **2. WayinVideo — Submit Transcription** submits the URL to the Transcription API
- **3. Wait — 90 Seconds** gives the API initial processing time
- **4. WayinVideo — Get Transcript Results** polls the transcripts results endpoint
- **5. IF — Transcription Complete?** checks for SUCCEEDED — if not, retries via 30-second wait
- **7. Code — Format Transcript** formats each segment with speaker label and timestamp, computes duration and word count
- **8. AI Agent — Extract Support Summary** uses GPT-4o-mini with 10 labeled output sections to extract structured support intelligence
- **9. OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** language model attached to the AI Agent
- **10. Code — Parse Support Output** extracts all 10 sections, maps priority to Zendesk values, builds the full ticket body with section headers
- **11. HTTP Request — Create Zendesk Ticket** POSTs to the Zendesk REST API with the customer as requester and parsed priority
- **12. Google Sheets — Log Support Record** appends 19 columns including Zendesk Ticket ID and constructed Ticket URL

## Set up steps
1. In **2** and **4** — replace `YOUR_WAYINVIDEO_API_KEY`
2. In **9. OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** — connect your OpenAI credential
3. In **11. HTTP Request — Create Zendesk Ticket** — replace `YOUR_ZENDESK_SUBDOMAIN`, `YOUR_ZENDESK_EMAIL`, and `YOUR_ZENDESK_API_TOKEN` (Zendesk Admin → Apps and Integrations → APIs → Add API Token)
4. In **12. Google Sheets — Log Support Record** — connect your Google Sheets OAuth2 credential and replace `YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID`
5. Create a Google Sheet tab named Support Call Log with columns: Call Date, Agent Name, Customer Name, Customer Email, Product, Issue Category, Issue Summary, Root Cause, Resolution Given, Follow-up Required, Sentiment, Priority, CSAT Prediction, Tags, Zendesk Ticket ID, Zendesk Ticket URL, Call Duration (min), Recording URL, Created On

## Form Input
Agent submits call recording URL, agent name, customer name, customer email, issue category, call date, and product or service name.

nodes.name: Section — WayinVideo Transcription Submit and Poll

parameters.content:
## WayinVideo Transcription Submit and Poll
Submits the call recording URL to the Transcription API. Waits 90 seconds for initial processing. Polls the results endpoint until status equals SUCCEEDED.

nodes.name: Section — Transcription Status Check and Retry Loop

parameters.content:
## Transcription Status Check and Retry Loop
IF checks for SUCCEEDED status. TRUE proceeds to transcript formatting. FALSE waits 30 seconds and polls again. Loop continues until transcript is ready.

nodes.name: Section — Transcript Formatting and AI Support Analysis

parameters.content:
## Transcript Formatting and AI Support Analysis
Formats transcript with speaker labels and timestamps. GPT-4o-mini extracts 10 structured fields: ticket subject, issue summary, root cause, resolution, follow-up, key quotes, sentiment, priority, CSAT prediction, and tags.

nodes.name: Section — Support Output Parse, Zendesk Ticket Create, and Sheet Log

parameters.content:
## Support Output Parse, Zendesk Ticket Create, and Sheet Log
Parses 10 labeled sections, maps priority to Zendesk values, builds ticket body. Creates Zendesk ticket via REST API with customer as requester. Logs 19 fields to Google Sheets including Ticket ID and URL.

parameters.formTitle: Support Call to Zendesk Ticket Creator

values.placeholder: e.g. Billing, Technical Issue, Onboarding, Account, Feature Request
e.g. Incrementors CRM, Enterprise Plan, Mobile App

parameters.formDescription: Paste your customer support call recording URL. AI will transcribe the full call, extract the issue summary, and automatically create a Zendesk ticket.

options.systemMessage:
=You are an expert customer support analyst who extracts structured support data from call transcripts.

Read this customer support call transcript and extract a complete structured support summary for the Zendesk ticket.

---

## CALL DETAILS
- Agent: {{ $json.agentName }}
- Customer: {{ $json.customerName }} ({{ $json.customerEmail }})
- Product: {{ $json.productName }}
- Issue Category: {{ $json.issueCategory }}
- Call Date: {{ $json.callDate }}
- Duration: {{ $json.totalMinutes }} minutes
- Speakers: {{ $json.speakers }}
- Recording: {{ $json.recordingUrl }}

---

## EXTRACTION RULES
1. Extract ONLY from what was actually said in the call
2. Be specific — vague summaries are not useful for support teams
3. Issue summary must describe the exact problem the customer reported
4. Root cause must explain WHY the problem happened (or 'Under investigation' if unclear)
5. Resolution must describe exactly what was done or said to resolve the issue
6. Follow-up must be specific actions with owners if mentioned in the call
7. Sentiment: Very Satisfied / Satisfied / Neutral / Frustrated / Very Frustrated
8. Priority: Urgent / High / Normal / Low — based on customer tone and issue severity
9. CSAT prediction: 1-5 based on how the call ended

---

## OUTPUT FORMAT
Return in this exact structure:

TICKET_SUBJECT:
[One line subject for the Zendesk ticket — 60-80 chars — clear and specific]

ISSUE_SUMMARY:
[2-3 sentences describing exactly what the customer reported]

ROOT_CAUSE:
[1-2 sentences explaining why the issue occurred — or 'Under investigation']

RESOLUTION_GIVEN:
[What the agent did or said to resolve the issue — or 'Unresolved — escalation required']

FOLLOW_UP_REQUIRED:
[Specific follow-up actions needed — or 'None']

KEY_QUOTES:
- [Most important thing the customer said]
- [Second key quote if available]

SENTIMENT:
[One of: Very Satisfied / Satisfied / Neutral / Frustrated / Very Frustrated]

PRIORITY:
[One of: Urgent / High / Normal / Low]

CSAT_PREDICTION:
[Number 1-5]

TAGS:
[5-8 lowercase tags relevant to this ticket — comma separated]
